chunks.text:
Instructions for Submitting Assignment Booklets

1. Submit Assignment Booklets regularly for correction.
2. Submit only one Assignment Booklet at one time. This allows your teacher to
provide helpful comments that you can apply to subsequent course work and
exams (if applicable).
3. Check the following before submitting each Assignment Booklet:
a. Are all assignments complete?
b. Have you edited your work to ensure accuracy of information and details?
c. Have you proofread your work to ensure correct grammar, spelling, and
punctuation?
d. Did you complete the Assignment Booklet cover and attach the correct label?

Postal Mail
Determine sufficient postage by having the envelope weighed at a post office.
(Envelopes less than two centimetres thick receive the most economical rate.)

Alternate Methods
Contact your teacher if you require an alternate method for submission of assignments.

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20
Assignment Booklet 3
Assessment

For successful completion of this course, you must

1.

Complete all questions in each Assignment Booklet to the best of your ability.

2.

Achieve at least 40% on the final exam.

3. Achieve a final course mark of at least 50%.

Process
•
Read the course material
 before working on the activities in this Assignment
Booklet.  The Module Book contains the information necessary for successful
completion of your work.

•
Proceed carefully
through each assignment.  Reflect upon your answers and prepare
your written responses to communicate your thoughts effectively.  Time spent in
planning results in better writing.

•
Proofread your work
 before submitting it for marking.  Check for content,
organization, paragraph construction (if applicable), grammar, spelling, and
punctuation.

•
If you encounter difficulties or have any questions
, contact your course teacher at
Alberta Distance Learning Centre for assistance.

Format
If you choose to
 use a word processor
for your written work,
• format your work using an easy-to-read 12-point serif font such as Times New Roman
• include your full name and student file number as a document header
• double-space your final copy
• staple your printed work to this Assignment Booklet

all submitted work must be your own

We look forward to assisting students who are sincere in their desire to learn.  Students may contact
their course teacher(s) by phone, e-mail, fax, postal mail, or in person at an Alberta Distance Learning

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 3

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  1

Value
 16
Part One:  Multiple-Choice Items

Read each item carefully and decide which choice best completes the statement or answers the
question.  Write the letter of your choice in the blank before the item.

 _B__ 1. According to Cattell, the two major types of intelligence are

 p47  A.  valid and reliable
   B.  fluid and crystallized
   C.  mathematical and verbal
   D.  mental and chronological

 _D__ 2. Which test provides an indicator of child’s emotional well-being?

 p49  A.  CogAT
   B.  WISC-III
   C.  Standford-Binet
   D.  Draw-A-Person test

 _A__ 3. Which of the following is not one of Gardner’s multiple intelligences?

 p46  A.  Social
   B.  Musical
   C.  Intrapersonal
   D.  Logical-mathematical

 _D__ 4. An IQ score measures

 p44  A.  a person’s intelligence
   B.  a person’s intellectual qualities
   C.  whether a person is gifted or developmentally delayed
   D.  how well a person has performed on an intelligence test

 _D__ 5. A test’s validity is how well the test

 p138  A.  measures intelligence
Glossary  B.  can predict future behaviour
   C.  can identify “gifted” children
   D.  measure what it is supposed to measure
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 3  Personal Psychology 20
2  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

_C__ 6. The Peabody Picture Vocabulary Test-Revised is used to

 p49  A.  determine a child’s reading level
   B.  identify children who should advance a grade
   C.  identify people with communication difficulties
   D.  determine a deaf child’s understanding of words

 _B__ 7. Which of the following does not make up part of the Triarchic Theory of
Intelligence?

 p45  A.  The way a person generates intelligent behaviour
   B.  The way a person visualizes solutions to problems
   C.  The degree to which person’s behaviour suits the situation
   D.  The person’s ability to analyze and apply appropriate behaviours in novel
      situations within a small time frame

 _B__ 8. The Weschler Intelligence Scale for Children (WISC-III)

 p48  A.  assess musical ability
   B.  gives a full-scale IQ score
   C.  is a group intelligence test
   D.  measures intelligence in babies

 _D__ 9. Intelligence tests often favour

 p50  A.  boys from poor families
   B.  girls from a variety of backgrounds
   C.  children from third-world countries
   D.  white children from middle-class families

 __C_ 10. Earl Hunt was interested in

 p46  A.  developing culture-fair IQ tests
   B.  correlations between IQ scores and family income
   C.  the unique methods people used to solve problems
   D.  the common methods people used to solve problems
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 3

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  3

 _A__ 11. An IQ score of 100 means the

 p44  A.  score is average
   B.  score is below average
   C.  score is above average
   D.  person taking the test answered all questions correctly

 _C__ 12. Sternberg believes

 p45  A.  there are seven different intelligences
   B.  the higher the IQ score, the smarter the person
   C.  how people use their intelligence is most important
   D.  crystallized and fluid intelligence are equally important

 _D__ 13. The zone of proximal development is the difference between

 p47  A.  IQ scores as a child ages
   B.  a child’s mathematical and verbal scores
   C.  the IQ scores of a developmentally delayed child and a gifted child
   D.  what a child can do on his/her own and what he/she can do with help

 _D__ 14. On average, a child will score higher on an intelligence test if he or she

 p52  A.  is an orphan
   B.  has many siblings
   C.  is the youngest in the family
   D.  is the older of two children in a family

 _A__ 15. According to Cattell, the type of intelligence a person is born with is_____
intelligence.

 p47  A.  fluid
   B.  naturalistic
   C.  crystallized
   D.  chronological

 _A__ 16. IQ is determined by _____ your _____ then multiplying by 100.

 p44  A.  dividing; mental age by your chronological age
   B.  dividing; chronological age by your mental age
   C.  multiplying; mental age with your chronological age
   D.  subtracting; chronological age from your mental age
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 3  Personal Psychology 20
4  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

Value
10
Part Two:  Matching – Gardner’s Intelligences

Choose the letter of the intelligence that is being described in each scenario.  Terms may be
used more than once.

Statements

Terms
 __I__ 1. Olivia smoothly parallel parks her car between
two trucks on a city street.
A.   bodily-kinesthetic
   B.   existential
p46    C.   interpersonal
 _B__ 2. Athena believes we are born to serve others. D.   intrapersonal
    E.   linguistic
 _E__ 3. Dianna loves to complete crossword puzzles in
her spare time.
F.   logical-mathematical
   G.   musical
    H.   naturalist
 _A__ 4. Jeffery enjoys his gymnastics classes. I.   spatial

 _F__ 5. Neil determines how much money he can invest
at the end of the month after paying his rent,
utilities, and grocery expenses.

 _H__ 6. Austin practices identifying flowers types while
hiking in Banff.

 _D__ 7. Mackenzie realizes she gets emotional at
weddings and funerals so she always brings
tissues to these events.

 _F__ 8. Payton calculates the degree to which the main
girder of the third section of the new bridge is to
be sloped to resist the stress from the horizontal
beam.

 _C__ 9. Beth and Ben work as mediators between groups
that are in contract negotiations.

 _G_ 10. Emily plays the flute, piano, and clarinet.

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 3

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  5

Value
7
Part Three:  True or False

Read each item carefully and decide if the statement is true or false.  Write T or F in the
blanks to indicate your choices.

 _F__ 1. The Flynn Effect refers to IQ decreasing with age.
 p52
 __T_ 2. The misuse of intelligence test can lead to labelling/stereotyping.
 p51
 _F__ 3. The number of years you have been alive is your mental age.
 p44
 _T__ 4. You can study for an intelligence test.
 p51
 _T__ 5. Determining intelligence strictly on IQ scores has become widely criticized.
 p44
 _T__ 6. Children taking the WISC-III may be asked to solve math problems orally.
 p48
 _F__ 7. Bias has been eliminated from IQ tests.
 p50

Value
17
Part Four:  Written-Response Items

Respond to the following items on the blanks below the items.

4
1. List four factors that could affect a person’s results on an intelligence test.

p51
Answers will vary.   Accept reasonable responses such as the following:

- experiencing culture bias in the test, coming from a disadvantage home (poverty)

- being tired, being hungry, being sick, being stressed, having had a recent tragedy

- being emotionally moody, etc.
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Assignment Booklet 3  Personal Psychology 20
6  ADLC Personal Psychology 20

4
2. Of Gardner’s Intelligences, which two do you think are your strongest?  Explain.

p46
Answers will vary.  Accept any reasonable response provide an explanation is given.

2
3. According to Cattell, what are “street smarts?”

p47
Street smarts refer to the type of intelligence not learned in school; it is the intelligence

a person is born with.

3
4. Using the internet, a dictionary, a knowledgeable friend, etc., define “idiot savant” and
provide an example.

An idiot savant is an intellectually challenged person (lower intelligence) who has

exceptional skill or brilliance is some limited field.  Examples will vary, but accept

most musical, mathematical, etc. examples students give.
chunks.titleGuess: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY

chunks.text:
Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 3

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  7

4
5. We assess the capabilities of people every day, sometimes consciously, sometimes
subconsciously.  Take a moment and think of the people you interact with regularly.
Who do you consider to be intelligent?  How did you come to this conclusion?

In a paragraph or two, detail how you determine someone to be intelligent – list a few
characteristics you assess intelligent people to have.

Answers will vary.  Give full marks for responses that highlight 2-3

areas/characteristics.

End of Assignment Booklet 3
After you have completed all items, submit your work to your marker.
